# Essay

I've read this many times over the years. I don't put many articles in my notes, but essays that I return to repeatedly deserve a spot among the books — especially essays digging into the many weaknesses in humanity's beloved codex as a learning tool, and our puzzling insistence on its continuation as *the* primary tool for sharing and gaining knowledge.

In short: *transmissionism* — the idea that somebody speaks or reads linear words describing some knowledge to you, and that knowledge is then in your brain, that their knowledge has been *transmitted* via seminar or book or whatever — is bunk. Empirically disproven top to bottom, not to mention obviously, intuitively false to anybody with a human brain who has ever tried to learn something. You just need Andy to boost you up on his shoulders so you can look down on the whole thing from the outside. Go do that:

<https://andymatuschak.org/books/>
